hypotheses.name: Incident/Outage Confirmed Impacting Customer
hypotheses.statement: Service team incident '{incident_id}' (Severity {severity}) by '{owning_tenant_name}' is confirmed impacting customer '{customer_name}' — SLI degradation detected in '{sli_category}' and other customers have reported issues. The customer has not raised an SR yet.
hypotheses.supporting_signals: Strongest proactive signal from incident path — incident + customer SLI degradation + other SRs. SYM-OUT-002 (confirmed outage) is the strongest trigger. SYM-OUT-003 (child incidents) or SYM-OUT-005 (multiple incidents) indicate widespread blast radius. The SLI category identifies how the customer is affected. SYM-SUP-006 (multi-customer CritSits) escalates severity further. Action: proactive customer notification recommended.

hypotheses.name: Incident/Outage Possibly Affecting Customer
hypotheses.statement: Service team incident '{incident_id}' by '{owning_tenant_name}' may be affecting customer '{customer_name}'. Partial evidence found — either the customer's SLIs show degradation OR other customers have reported issues, but not both.
hypotheses.supporting_signals: Incident exists with partial corroboration. If SYM-SLI-001 fires (customer SLI degraded) but no other SRs — the incident may be causing isolated customer impact. If SYM-SUP-005 fires (other SRs) but no SLI spike for this customer — the incident is affecting others and this customer may be next or may have different exposure to the failure. Monitor for escalation.

hypotheses.name: Incident/Outage Detected — No Customer Impact Evidence
hypotheses.statement: Service team incident '{incident_id}' by '{owning_tenant_name}' exists but no evidence of customer '{customer_name}' impact found — no SLI degradation, no other customer SRs, and the customer hasn't reported issues.
hypotheses.supporting_signals: Incident detected but no customer impact evidence. This could mean: (1) the incident affects a different region/service than this customer uses, (2) the incident is being mitigated before customer impact, (3) early stage — customer impact may emerge later. SYM-OUT-002 (confirmed outage) increases risk even without current customer impact. Monitor the customer's SLIs for emerging degradation.

hypotheses.name: Incident Not Impacting Customer
hypotheses.statement: Service team incident '{incident_id}' by '{owning_tenant_name}' has no measurable impact on customer '{customer_name}'. The incident exists but is either resolved, mitigated, or affects different regions/services than the customer uses.
hypotheses.supporting_signals: This is a negative conclusion. The incident exists (SYM-OUT-001) but is NOT a confirmed outage (no SYM-OUT-002), the customer's SLIs are clean (no SYM-SLI-001), no one has filed SRs (no SYM-SUP-001/005), and no child incidents (no SYM-OUT-003). The incident is either resolved, mitigated, or scoped to a region/service the customer doesn't use.
